name: Cabo Tramuntana, Cabo Llebeig y na Pòpia — the lighthouses of Sa Dragonera Natural Park
name_original: Cabo Tramuntana, Cabo Llebeig y na Pòpia — los faros del Parque Natural de Sa Dragonera
region_intro: Easy hiking trails in Sa Dragonera explore a protected natural park characterized by rugged terrain, steep cliffs, and Mediterranean vegetation. This uninhabited island, a geological continuation of the Serra de Tramuntana, offers dramatic coastal views and unique biodiversity. Its highest point, Pico Popi, reaches approximately 360 meters, providing a varied landscape for hikers. The island is renowned for its endemic Balearic lizards and serves as a crucial breeding ground for numerous bird species. Best easy hiking trails in Sa Dragonera The most popular easy hiking route is View of Sa Dragonera – View of Sa Dragonera Island loop from Es Pantaleu, a 2.2 miles (3.5 km) trail that takes 1 hour 4 minutes to complete. This route offers panoramic views of the island's distinctive silhouette. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Sa Dragonera Island – View of Sant Elm loop from Es Pantaleu, an easy 3.5 miles (5.6 km) path. This trail provides impressive vistas of both Sa Dragonera and the Mallorcan coast near Sant Elm. Local hikers also love the Sant Elm Beach – Sant Elm Beach loop from Es Pantaleu, a 3.6 miles (5.8 km) trail leading through coastal scenery, often completed in about 1 hour 37 minutes. Hiking in Sa Dragonera is defined by rugged coastal paths, Mediterranean vegetation, and historical lighthouses. The network offers options primarily for easy hiking trails suitable for various ability levels. The routes in Sa Dragonera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10,000 reviews. More than 40,000 hikers have used komoot to explore Sa Dragonera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are there in Sa Dragonera?
A: Sa Dragonera offers a wide selection of easy hiking trails, with over 100 routes suitable for various ability levels. These trails allow you to explore the island's unique natural park and its stunning coastal landscapes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints on easy hikes in Sa Dragonera?
A: Easy hikes on Sa Dragonera often lead to impressive viewpoints and historical landmarks. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360904" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Far de Tramuntana Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;, which offers excellent views of the Mallorcan coast. Another popular spot is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/188423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Mediterranean Sea from the Cliffs of Es Tancat&lt;/a&gt;, providing dramatic coastal vistas. The island also features the ruins of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5360911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Lighthouse of Na Popia&lt;/a&gt; at its highest point, though the path to it is considered medium difficulty.

Q: Are there any circular easy walks in Sa Dragonera?
A: Yes, Sa Dragonera features several easy circular walks. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31732821" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Sa Dragonera – View of Sa Dragonera Island loop from Es Pantaleu&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.5 km long and offers panoramic views of the island's distinctive silhouette. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278500" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sa Dragonera Island – Far de Tramuntana Lighthouse loop from sa Dragonera&lt;/a&gt;, a 5.9 km route that takes you to the lighthouse and back.

Q: What is the best time of year for easy hikes in Sa Dragonera?
A: The best time for easy hikes in Sa Dragonera is typically during the spring (April to June) and autumn (September to October). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making it ideal for exploring. The island's unique flora is particularly vibrant in spring, and autumn offers opportunities for bird watching, especially for ospreys.

Q: What kind of wildlife can I expect to see on easy hikes in Sa Dragonera?
A: Sa Dragonera is a haven for wildlife. You'll frequently encounter the endemic Balearic lizard (Podarcis lilfordi ssp. giglioli), known for its tameness. The island is also a crucial breeding ground for numerous bird species, including Audouin's gull, European shag, Eleonora's falcon, and peregrine falcon. Ospreys and other seabirds can often be spotted soaring above the cliffs.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in Sa Dragonera family-friendly?
A: Yes, many of the easy hiking trails in Sa Dragonera are suitable for families. The island's well-marked paths and relatively short distances make them enjoyable for children. The opportunity to see unique wildlife like the Balearic lizards and explore historical lighthouses adds to the family appeal. Remember that access to the island is by boat, which can be an adventure in itself.

Q: Can I bring my dog on easy hikes in Sa Dragonera?
A: As Sa Dragonera is a protected Natural Park, specific regulations apply to pets to preserve its unique ecosystem. It is generally not permitted to bring dogs onto the island to protect the native wildlife, especially ground-nesting birds and the endemic Balearic lizard. It's always best to check the latest park regulations before planning your visit.

Q: How do I get to Sa Dragonera for hiking, and what about parking?
A: Access to Sa Dragonera is exclusively by boat. Ferries depart regularly from nearby towns like Sant Elm and Port d'Andratx on Mallorca. Once you arrive at Cala Lladó on Sa Dragonera, all hiking trails start from there. If you're driving to Sant Elm or Port d'Andratx, you'll find public parking available in these towns before taking the ferry.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Sa Dragonera?
A: The hiking experience in Sa Dragonera is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10,000 reviews. Hikers often praise the island's untouched natural beauty, the dramatic coastal views, the unique wildlife encounters, and the well-maintained trails that make exploration accessible. The sense of being in a protected, wild environment is a common highlight.

Q: Are there any amenities or places to eat near the easy hiking trails on Sa Dragonera?
A: Sa Dragonera is an uninhabited natural park, so amenities on the island itself are very limited. There is a ranger station and an interpretation center at Cala Lladó where ferries arrive, but no shops or restaurants. It is essential to bring your own water, snacks, and any other supplies you might need. For cafes, pubs, and accommodation, you will need to return to Sant Elm or Port d'Andratx on Mallorca.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on easy hiking trails in Sa Dragonera?
A: Even easy trails on Sa Dragonera feature rugged terrain, as the island is a geological continuation of the Serra de Tramuntana. You can expect paths with some elevation changes, rocky sections, and coastal trails offering dramatic views of steep cliffs. The vegetation is typically Mediterranean, with drought-tolerant species like rosemary and wild olive. While easy, sturdy footwear is recommended.
tips:
Dragonera island lighthouse, at a distance of approximately 700 meters from the island of Majorca.
It is one of the two lighthouses on Dragonera Island that replaced the old Na Popia lighthouse.
At 349 meters above sea level, the old lighthouse of the island, which is no longer used today, stands on the summit.

Lighthouse located in the north of Dragonera Island, 700 meters from the island of Mallorca, Spain.4
It was the second lighthouse to be built on that island, due to the extinct Na Popia lighthouse.

Especially funny and beautiful to watch are the small Balearic lizards that exist on Sa Dragonera in masses. The little dragons are very nimble and curious. The bags should therefore be kept well closed.

waypoints.name: Ruins of the Old Lighthouse of Na Popia (349 m)
